demo-backendservice1/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Maven compiler plugin version to a newer snapshot release.
Change the Maven compiler release property from Java 17 to Java 21.
demo-backendservice1/pom.xml.why_exists: Compilation will fail or produce bytecode for Java 17 instead of the target Java 21 if this property is not updated.

demo-backendservice1/pom.xml.general_idea: Explicitly set the release version to 21 in the maven-compiler-plugin configuration.
demo-backendservice1/pom.xml.why_exists: Ensures the compiler targets Java 21 bytecode, overriding or reinforcing the property setting.

demo-service1/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Maven compiler plugin version to a newer snapshot compatible with Java 21.
Change the Maven compiler release property from Java 17 to Java 21.
demo-service1/pom.xml.why_exists: Code targeting Java 21 features or APIs will fail to compile if the compiler release is set to 17.

demo-service1/pom.xml.general_idea: Explicitly set the release configuration in the maven-compiler-plugin to 21.
demo-service1/pom.xml.why_exists: Ensures the compiler plugin explicitly targets Java 21, overriding or reinforcing the property setting.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update Maven compiler source and target properties from Java 17 to Java 21 to align with the new JDK version.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project would compile against Java 17 language features and bytecode, failing to utilize Java 21 features or potentially causing compatibility issues if Java 21-specific APIs are used in the codebase.
